name: functions
context: Run serverless backend code triggered by HTTP requests, Firestore events, Auth events, Storage events, and scheduled jobs using Cloud Functions for Firebase
info: Cloud Functions for Firebase (2nd gen) run on Cloud Run and provide event-driven serverless compute tightly integrated with Firebase services. Trigger types include onRequest and onCall for HTTP, onDocumentCreated/Updated/Deleted/Written for Firestore, onAuthUserCreated/Deleted for Authentication, onObjectFinalized for Cloud Storage, onSchedule for cron jobs, and onMessagePublished for Pub/Sub. Functions are written in TypeScript, JavaScript, or Python, live in the functions/ directory, and export from src/index.ts. The v2 API uses subpath imports: firebase-functions/v2/https, firebase-functions/v2/firestore, firebase-functions/v2/auth, firebase-functions/v2/storage, firebase-functions/v2/scheduler, firebase-functions/v2/pubsub. Environment configuration uses defineSecret() for secrets from Google Secret Manager and defineString()/defineInt() for parameterized config. The Firebase Emulator Suite runs functions locally on port 5001 for testing against emulated Firestore, Auth, and Storage. Deploy with firebase deploy --only functions or target individual functions with --only functions:functionName. Cold starts are mitigated with minInstances. 2nd gen functions support up to 32 GiB memory, 60-minute timeout, and configurable concurrency (multiple requests per instance). Set region explicitly for production. See [[firebase/cli]] for CLI setup and [[firebase/config]] for firebase.json configuration.

sections.title: HTTP functions
sections.body: HTTP functions use `onRequest` from `firebase-functions/v2/https` and receive Express-compatible `(req, res)` arguments. They deploy to a public URL at `https://REGION-PROJECT.cloudfunctions.net/FUNCTION_NAME`. Set `cors: true` in options for browser access. Use `--only functions:name` to deploy individually. For authenticated client calls, use `onCall` instead — it automatically parses the Firebase Auth token.

sections.title: Callable functions
sections.body: Callable functions use `onCall` from `firebase-functions/v2/https`. The client SDK invokes them via `httpsCallable(functions, 'name')`. The `request.auth` object is automatically populated with the caller's UID, email, and token claims. Throw `HttpsError` for structured error responses. Callable functions handle CORS, auth token validation, and request/response serialization automatically.

sections.title: Firestore triggers
sections.body: Firestore triggers fire when documents change. `onDocumentCreated('path/{id}', handler)` fires on new documents. `onDocumentUpdated` provides `event.data.before` and `event.data.after` snapshots. `onDocumentDeleted` fires when a document is removed. `onDocumentWritten` fires on any change (create, update, delete). Path parameters like `{userId}` are available in `event.params`. Import from `firebase-functions/v2/firestore`.

sections.title: Auth triggers
sections.body: `onAuthUserCreated` fires when a new user signs up (any provider). `onAuthUserDeleted` fires when a user account is deleted. The `event.data` object contains the user record (uid, email, displayName, photoURL, etc.). Import from `firebase-functions/v2/identity`. Common uses: create user profile documents, send welcome emails, clean up user data on deletion.

sections.title: Storage triggers
sections.body: `onObjectFinalized` fires when a file upload completes or an existing object is overwritten. The `event.data` object contains `name` (file path), `bucket`, `contentType`, `size`, and `metadata`. Import from `firebase-functions/v2/storage`. Use for image processing, virus scanning, metadata extraction, or triggering downstream workflows on file upload.

sections.title: Scheduled functions
sections.body: `onSchedule` from `firebase-functions/v2/scheduler` runs functions on a cron schedule. Accepts App Engine cron syntax ('every 5 minutes', 'every day 02:00') or standard cron expressions ('0 9 * * 1'). Backed by Cloud Scheduler. Use for cleanup jobs, report generation, data sync, and digest emails. Set `timeoutSeconds` appropriately for long-running scheduled tasks.

sections.title: Environment and secrets configuration
sections.body: Use `defineSecret('NAME')` from `firebase-functions/params` for sensitive values stored in Google Secret Manager. Use `defineString('NAME')` and `defineInt('NAME')` for non-secret config with defaults. Secrets must be declared in the function's options object (`{ secrets: [mySecret] }`). Non-secret env vars go in `functions/.env` (deployed) or `functions/.env.local` (emulator only). Access values with `.value()` at runtime.

sections.title: Deployment and emulator
sections.body: `firebase deploy --only functions` deploys all exported functions from src/index.ts. Target specific functions with `--only functions:name`. The emulator (`firebase emulators:start --only functions`) runs on port 5001 and integrates with emulated Firestore, Auth, and Storage. View logs with `firebase functions:log`. Delete functions with `firebase functions:delete name`. Functions not exported from index.ts are automatically deleted on deploy.

links.context: Firebase platform overview — project setup, client SDK, admin SDK
Cloud Firestore — the database that triggers onDocumentCreated/Updated/Deleted
Firebase Auth — triggers onAuthUserCreated/Deleted, provides request.auth in callables
Cloud Storage — triggers onObjectFinalized on file uploads
Firebase CLI — firebase init functions, firebase deploy, firebase emulators
firebase.json configuration — functions deploy targets and emulator ports

related.note: Firebase project must be initialized before adding Cloud Functions
Firestore document events are a primary trigger source for Cloud Functions
Google Cloud Functions is the underlying platform — Firebase Functions adds Firebase-specific triggers and tooling

scripts.name: Create Firebase Cloud Function
scripts.description: Generate a Cloud Function with the correct v2 API boilerplate for the specified trigger type

FUNCTION_NAME.description: Name of the function to create

TRIGGER.description: Trigger type: http, callable, firestore, auth, storage, schedule

functionName.description: Name of the Cloud Function to create or deploy

minInstances.description: Minimum instances to keep warm (0 = scale to zero)

functionUrl.description: HTTP URL of the deployed function (HTTP/callable only)

depends.reason: Firebase CLI is required for init, deploy, and emulator commands
firebase.json must be configured with functions deploy targets

author: dojo-community
